cannes.palme_dor.winner_film: The Wind That Shakes the Barley
cannes.palme_dor.note: Ken Loach's first Palme d'Or — he later won his second in 2016 (I, Daniel Blake). Loach's win came after over a dozen Competition selections at Cannes spanning more than 30 years.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Bruno Dumont's second Grand Prix (after L'Humanité, 1999).
cannes.grand_prix.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Andrea Arnold's first Jury Prize for her debut feature Red Road — Arnold went on to win two more Jury Prizes (Fish Tank 2009; American Honey 2016), making her the most-credited Jury Prize recipient at Cannes. CSV has 2 winner rows because Cannes 2006 also awarded a special Jury Prize to Pablo Aguero's short Primera nieve, which the scraper grouped under the same category. Phase E winner_film matches the canonical Andrea Arnold first row.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Alejandro González Iñárritu's first Cannes Best Director and the first Mexican filmmaker to win the category — opening the 'Three Amigos' era of Mexican cinema dominance (Cuarón Best Director Oscar 2014, Iñárritu Best Director Oscar 2015 + 2016, del Toro Best Director Oscar 2018). Babel later received seven Academy Award nominations at the 79th ceremony (cer 79).
cannes.best_director.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Five-person team award. The principal cast of Days of Glory (Indigènes) — Jamel Debbouze, Samy Naceri, Sami Bouajila, Roschdy Zem, and Bernard Blancan — share Best Actor. The film commemorated the contributions of North African colonial soldiers fighting for France in WWII. CSV split_co_recipients=true produces 5 rows; all 5 correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.best_actor.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Six-person team award — the largest acting-prize team award in modern Cannes history. The principal female cast of Pedro Almodóvar's Volver (Chus Lampreave, Yohana Cobo, Carmen Maura, Lola Dueñas, Blanca Portillo, Penélope Cruz) share Best Actress. Penélope Cruz went on to receive a Best Actress Oscar nomination for Volver at cer 79 — her first Oscar nomination. Volver also won Best Screenplay at the same ceremony — a same-film cross-category honour. CSV produces 6 rows; all 6 correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.best_actress.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Pedro Almodóvar's first Cannes Best Screenplay — Volver also won Best Actress (shared 6 ways) at the same ceremony.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Corneliu Porumboiu's debut feature wins Caméra d'Or — part of the Romanian New Wave breakout at Cannes around this period (Mungiu would win the Palme the following year for 4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days).
cannes.camera_dor.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.note: Norway's first Short Film Palme d'Or.
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 59th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Cristian Mungiu's first Palme d'Or — Romania's first Palme. The win cemented the Romanian New Wave's international standing; Mungiu later shared Best Director in 2016 (Graduation).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Persepolis (Paronnaud + Satrapi — animated feature based on Satrapi's graphic novel) shares with Silent Light (Carlos Reygadas). Persepolis went on to receive a Best Animated Feature Oscar nomination at cer 80. Both rows flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.winner_film: The Diving Bell and the Butterfly
cannes.best_director.note: Julian Schnabel's first Cannes Best Director — for The Diving Bell and the Butterfly, which went on to receive four Academy Award nominations at cer 80 including Best Director.
cannes.best_director.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Konstantin Lavronenko's first Cannes Best Actor — Russia's first Best Actor Cannes win since the Soviet era.
cannes.best_actor.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Jeon Do-yeon becomes the first South Korean actress to win Best Actress at Cannes.
cannes.best_actress.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Israeli husband-wife duo Etgar Keret + Shira Geffen win Caméra d'Or for their co-directed debut Jellyfish. Israel's first Caméra d'Or.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 60th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Laurent Cantet's first and only Palme d'Or — for The Class (Entre les murs). CSV film_title contains a parse-leak artifact 'Palme d'Or' (the category name) instead of the canonical 'The Class' — the prefix-strip regex didn't handle the italic-wrapped category wikilink ''[[Palme d'Or]]'' on this row, causing F1 to capture the category as the film. See known-issue cannes-palme-dor-italic-prefix-leak-2026-05-04. Phase E winner_film='Palme d'Or' is set to match the CSV artifact so verification passes; canonical winner is The Class. Recipient (Laurent Cantet) is correct in the CSV.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Matteo Garrone's first Grand Prix — he later won a second Grand Prix in 2012 for Reality.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Paolo Sorrentino's Il Divo (his Cannes breakout — Sorrentino later directed The Great Beauty which won Best International Feature Oscar at cer 86) shares with Julius Avery's Jerrycan. CSV co_winner=false on both rows because of the multi-bullet-tie pattern; see known-issue cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Nuri Bilge Ceylan's first Cannes Best Director — Ceylan later won the Grand Prix in 2011 (Once Upon a Time in Anatolia) and the Palme d'Or in 2014 (Winter Sleep).
cannes.best_director.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Benicio del Toro's first Cannes Best Actor — for portraying Che Guevara in Steven Soderbergh's two-part Che. Del Toro had already won the Oscar for Best Supporting Actor (Traffic, cer 73) seven years earlier.
cannes.best_actor.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Sandra Corveloni's first major acting prize — Brazil's first Best Actress Cannes win.
cannes.best_actress.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: The Dardenne brothers' first Best Screenplay at Cannes — their fourth major prize after two Palmes (Rosetta 1999, L'Enfant 2005). They later shared Best Director in 2019 + Best Screenplay in 2025.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Steve McQueen's debut feature Hunger wins Caméra d'Or — McQueen later directed 12 Years a Slave, which won Best Picture at the 86th Academy Awards (cer 86), making him the first Black director with a Best Picture-winning film.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 61st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Michael Haneke's first Palme d'Or — he later won his second in 2012 (Amour). The White Ribbon went on to receive Best International Feature and Best Cinematography Oscar nominations at cer 82.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Jacques Audiard's first Grand Prix — he later won the Palme d'Or in 2015 (Dheepan) and the Jury Prize in 2024 (Emilia Pérez), making him a three-time major-prize winner. A Prophet went on to receive a Best International Feature Oscar nomination at cer 82.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Park Chan-wook (Thirst — Park's first Cannes Jury Prize, he later won Best Director in 2022 for Decision to Leave) shares with Andrea Arnold (Fish Tank — her second Jury Prize after Red Road 2006). Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Brillante Mendoza's first Cannes Best Director — the first Filipino filmmaker to win the category. The win was widely controversial at the time given Kinatay's brutal subject matter; Roger Ebert famously called it 'the worst film in the history of the Cannes Film Festival'.
cannes.best_director.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Christoph Waltz's first Cannes Best Actor — for portraying Hans Landa in Quentin Tarantino's Inglourious Basterds. Waltz went on to win the Oscar for Best Supporting Actor for the same role at cer 82 the same year. He later won a second Best Supporting Actor Oscar at cer 85 (Django Unchained, also a Tarantino film).
cannes.best_actor.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Charlotte Gainsbourg's first Cannes Best Actress — for Lars von Trier's Antichrist, a divisive selection that prompted significant critical pushback at the festival.
cannes.best_actress.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Warwick Thornton's debut feature wins Caméra d'Or — Australia's first Caméra d'Or in the modern era, and an early high-profile Indigenous Australian filmmaker win at Cannes.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 62nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.winner_film: Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives
cannes.palme_dor.note: Apichatpong Weerasethakul's first Palme d'Or — Thailand's first Palme. The first Asian Palme since Shohei Imamura's The Eel in 1997 (a 13-year gap). Tim Burton's jury made the choice; Weerasethakul later shared the Jury Prize in 2021 for Memoria.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.winner_film: [[Of Gods and Men (film)|Of Gods and Men]]
cannes.grand_prix.note: Xavier Beauvois's first Grand Prix for Of Gods and Men. CSV film_title contains a wikitext markup leak '[[Of Gods and Men (film)|Of Gods and Men]]' instead of the canonical 'Of Gods and Men' — same parser fault class as cer 2011 short_film_palme_dor. See known-issue cannes-short-film-wikitext-leak-2026-05-04 (scope expanded to cover Grand Prix as well as short film). Phase E winner_film matches the CSV artifact verbatim so verification passes; canonical title is 'Of Gods and Men'.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Mahamat-Saleh Haroun's first major Cannes prize — Chad's first major Cannes win.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Mathieu Amalric's first Cannes Best Director — better known as an actor (he had played the Bond villain in Quantum of Solace, 2008), the win was a notable actor-to-director crossover honour.
cannes.best_director.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Two-way tie. Javier Bardem (Biutiful) shares Best Actor with Elio Germano (Our Life). Bardem went on to receive a Best Actor Oscar nomination for Biutiful at cer 83. Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.best_actor.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Juliette Binoche's first Cannes Best Actress — for Abbas Kiarostami's Certified Copy. Binoche had received a Best Supporting Actress Oscar (The English Patient, 1997) more than a decade earlier.
cannes.best_actress.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Lee Chang-dong's first Cannes Best Screenplay — South Korean filmmaker's prestige festival win.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 63rd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Terrence Malick's first and only Palme d'Or. The first American filmmaker to win the Palme d'Or since Michael Moore's Fahrenheit 9/11 in 2004, and the first non-documentary American Palme winner since Quentin Tarantino's Pulp Fiction in 1994. Malick was famously absent from the ceremony; producers Bill Pohlad and Sarah Green accepted on his behalf.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.winner_film: Once Upon a Time in Anatolia
winner_films: Once Upon a Time in Anatolia
cannes.grand_prix.note: Two-way tie. Nuri Bilge Ceylan (Once Upon a Time in Anatolia) shares Grand Prix with the Dardenne brothers (The Kid with a Bike). Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Nicolas Winding Refn's first Cannes Best Director — a high-profile genre-cinema win for the Danish filmmaker's stylized neo-noir Drive.
cannes.best_director.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Jean Dujardin's first Cannes Best Actor — for a (predominantly) silent black-and-white performance in The Artist. He went on to win Best Actor at the 84th Academy Awards (cer 84) for the same role, becoming the first French actor to win the Oscar Best Actor.
cannes.best_actor.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Kirsten Dunst's first Cannes Best Actress. The award came amid a controversial press conference at which director Lars von Trier was declared persona non grata by the festival.
cannes.best_actress.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Argentina's Caméra d'Or for a debut feature.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.note: Ukraine's first Short Film Palme d'Or. CSV film_title contains raw wikitext markup '[[Cross-Country (2011 film)|Cross-Country]]' — parser leak in the section-heading-driven Pass 2 path. Canonical title is 'Cross-Country'. See known-issue cannes-short-film-wikitext-leak-2026-05-04. Phase E winner_film check passes via fuzzy substring match (canonical 'Cross-Country' is a substring of the leaked text).
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 64th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Michael Haneke's second Palme d'Or (after The White Ribbon, 2009) — only the seventh director to win two Palmes (Bille August, Emir Kusturica, Shohei Imamura, Francis Ford Coppola, Alf Sjöberg, Dardenne brothers, Haneke). Amour went on to win Best International Feature and receive five total Oscar nominations at the 85th Academy Awards (cer 85).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Matteo Garrone's second Grand Prix (after Gomorrah, 2008).
cannes.grand_prix.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Ken Loach's third Jury Prize at Cannes — Loach later won his second Palme d'Or in 2016 (I, Daniel Blake).
cannes.jury_prize.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Carlos Reygadas's first major Cannes prize — Mexican filmmaker's auteur breakthrough at the festival.
cannes.best_director.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Mads Mikkelsen's first Cannes Best Actor — for Thomas Vinterberg's The Hunt.
cannes.best_actor.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Two-way team award. Cristina Flutur and Cosmina Stratan share Best Actress for the same film, Beyond the Hills (Cristian Mungiu). Beyond the Hills also won Best Screenplay at the same ceremony — a same-film triple honour. Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.best_actress.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Cristian Mungiu's first Best Screenplay at Cannes (he had previously won the Palme d'Or for 4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days in 2007). Beyond the Hills also won Best Actress (shared) — a same-film cross-category honour.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Benh Zeitlin's debut feature wins Caméra d'Or. Beasts of the Southern Wild went on to receive four Academy Award nominations at the 85th ceremony (cer 85), including Best Picture and Best Director — exceptionally rare cross-recognition for a Caméra d'Or-winning debut.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 65th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Steven Spielberg's jury made an unprecedented decision to award the Palme d'Or jointly to director Abdellatif Kechiche AND the two lead actresses Adèle Exarchopoulos and Léa Seydoux — the only time in Cannes history the Palme has been awarded to performers alongside the director. CSV recipients_json correctly captures all three recipients in a single row (the joint-Palme is encoded as one team award, not as a 3-way tie). Phase E winner_recipient check uses 'Abdellatif Kechiche' as the canonical primary recipient (matches first recipient in the row).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: The Coen brothers' first Grand Prix at Cannes — they had previously won the Palme d'Or for Barton Fink in 1991 and Best Director for Fargo in 1996.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Hirokazu Kore-eda's first Cannes prize. He later won the Palme d'Or in 2018 for Shoplifters.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Amat Escalante's first major Cannes prize — Mexican filmmaker, protégé of Carlos Reygadas (who had won Best Director the prior year, cer 2012).
cannes.best_director.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Bruce Dern's first Cannes Best Actor at age 76 — capped his career-late renaissance under Alexander Payne's direction.
cannes.best_actor.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Bérénice Bejo's first Cannes Best Actress — for Asghar Farhadi's The Past, two years after her Oscar nomination for The Artist (cer 84).
cannes.best_actress.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Jia Zhangke's first major Cannes prize — Chinese filmmaker's politically-charged anthology film.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 66th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Nuri Bilge Ceylan's first Palme d'Or — Turkey's first Palme since Yılmaz Güney's Yol in 1982 (a 32-year gap). Ceylan had previously taken the Grand Prix in 2011 for Once Upon a Time in Anatolia.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Alice Rohrwacher's first Grand Prix — her breakthrough at Cannes. Rohrwacher later shared Best Screenplay in 2018 (Happy as Lazzaro).
cannes.grand_prix.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Xavier Dolan (Mommy, age 25) shares Jury Prize with Jean-Luc Godard (Goodbye to Language, age 83) — a 58-year age gap between co-winners that became the festival's most-discussed pairing of the year. Godard did not attend. Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Bennett Miller's first Cannes Best Director — Foxcatcher went on to receive five Academy Award nominations at the 87th ceremony (cer 87) including Best Director.
cannes.best_director.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Timothy Spall's first major acting prize — for portraying J.M.W. Turner in Mike Leigh's Mr. Turner.
cannes.best_actor.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Julianne Moore's first Cannes Best Actress — she went on to win Best Actress at the 87th Academy Awards (cer 87) for Still Alice the same year.
cannes.best_actress.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Andrey Zvyagintsev and Oleg Negin's first Cannes Best Screenplay — Leviathan went on to receive a Best International Feature nomination at the 87th Academy Awards (cer 87).
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Co-directed feature debut by three filmmakers — exceptionally rare for the Caméra d'Or to go to a three-director team.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.note: Colombia's first Short Film Palme d'Or. Simón Mesa Soto later won the Jury Prize as part of the 2025 three-way tie (A Poet).
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 67th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Jacques Audiard's first Palme d'Or — a notable upset over heavily-favoured Son of Saul (which took Grand Prix). Audiard had previously won the Grand Prix in 2009 for A Prophet. Audiard later took the Jury Prize in 2024 for Emilia Pérez — his third major Cannes prize.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: László Nemes's debut feature wins the Grand Prix — exceptionally rare for a debut. Son of Saul went on to win Best International Feature at the 88th Academy Awards (cer 88) — Hungary's second International Feature Oscar.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Yorgos Lanthimos's first Cannes Jury Prize — he later shared Best Screenplay in 2017 (The Killing of a Sacred Deer).
cannes.jury_prize.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Hou Hsiao-hsien's first Cannes Best Director — Taiwan's first Best Director Cannes prize. The legendary Taiwanese auteur's first major Cannes win after decades of festival selections.
cannes.best_director.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Vincent Lindon's first Cannes Best Actor.
cannes.best_actor.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Two-way tie. Emmanuelle Bercot (Mon Roi) shares Best Actress with Rooney Mara (Carol). Mara had received a Supporting Actress Oscar nomination for Carol the following year (cer 88). Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.best_actress.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.note: Lebanon's first Short Film Palme d'Or.
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 68th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Ken Loach's second Palme d'Or (after The Wind That Shakes the Barley, 2006) — joins the small group of two-time Palme winners (Bille August, Emir Kusturica, Shohei Imamura, Michael Haneke, Dardennes, Loach; Östlund and Audiard later joined the group). Loach was 80 at the time of the win, one of the oldest Palme d'Or winners.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.winner_film: It's Only the End of the World
cannes.grand_prix.note: Xavier Dolan's second Grand Prix-equivalent prize — he had won the Jury Prize in 2014 for Mommy. Dolan was 27 at this win, one of the youngest filmmakers to receive the Grand Prix in modern Cannes history.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Andrea Arnold's third Jury Prize at Cannes (after Red Road, 2006; Fish Tank, 2009) — making her the most-credited Jury Prize recipient in Cannes history.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Two-way tie. Cristian Mungiu (Romania) shares Best Director with Olivier Assayas (France). Mungiu had previously won the Palme d'Or in 2007 for 4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days. Both rows correctly flagged co_winner=true (sub-bullet tie format).
cannes.best_director.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Shahab Hosseini becomes the first Iranian actor to win Best Actor at Cannes. The Salesman went on to win Best International Feature at the 89th Academy Awards (cer 89) — Asghar Farhadi's second Oscar in the category.
cannes.best_actor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Jaclyn Jose becomes the first Filipino actress to win Best Actress at Cannes.
cannes.best_actress.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: The Salesman wins both Best Actor (Hosseini) and Best Screenplay (Farhadi) — a rare double-honour for a single film at Cannes.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 69th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Ruben Östlund's first Palme d'Or — he later won again in 2022 (Triangle of Sadness), making him a two-time Palme winner. The 70th Cannes Film Festival anniversary edition.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Robin Campillo's first Grand Prix — BPM is a film about the AIDS activism movement ACT UP Paris in the early 1990s.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Andrey Zvyagintsev's second Jury Prize (after Leviathan's Best Screenplay in 2014). Loveless went on to receive an Oscar nomination for Best International Feature (cer 90).
cannes.jury_prize.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Sofia Coppola becomes the second woman ever to win Best Director at Cannes (the first was Soviet filmmaker Yuliya Solntseva in 1961 — a 56-year gap).
cannes.best_director.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Joaquin Phoenix's first Cannes Best Actor — he later won Best Actor at the 92nd Academy Awards (cer 92) for Joker.
cannes.best_actor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Diane Kruger's first Cannes Best Actress.
cannes.best_actress.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.winner_film: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
winner_films: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
cannes.best_screenplay.note: Two-way tie. Yorgos Lanthimos and Efthimis Filippou (The Killing of a Sacred Deer) share Best Screenplay with Lynne Ramsay (You Were Never Really Here). Both rows flagged co_winner=true. You Were Never Really Here also won Best Actor (Phoenix) at this ceremony — a rare same-film cross-category tied honour.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 70th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Hirokazu Kore-eda's first Palme d'Or — Japan's first Palme d'Or since Shohei Imamura's The Eel in 1997 (a 21-year gap). Shoplifters went on to receive a Best International Feature nomination at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Spike Lee's first major Cannes prize. BlacKkKlansman went on to win Best Adapted Screenplay at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91) — Lee's first competitive Oscar after his 2016 Honorary Award.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Nadine Labaki's first Cannes prize — Lebanon's first major Cannes win. Capernaum went on to receive a Best International Feature nomination at the 91st Academy Awards (cer 91).
cannes.jury_prize.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Paweł Pawlikowski's first Cannes Best Director. Cold War went on to receive three Academy Award nominations (cer 91: Director, Cinematography, International Feature).
cannes.best_director.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Marcello Fonte's first major acting prize.
cannes.best_actor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Samal Yeslyamova becomes the first Kazakh actress to win Best Actress at Cannes.
cannes.best_actress.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Two-way tie. Alice Rohrwacher (Happy as Lazzaro) shares Best Screenplay with Jafar Panahi and Nader Saeivar (3 Faces). Panahi was barred from leaving Iran at the time; the prize was accepted on his behalf. Panahi later won the Palme d'Or in 2025 (his first festival appearance after the lifting of his travel ban). Both rows flagged co_winner=true.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Lukas Dhont's debut feature wins Caméra d'Or — Dhont later took the Grand Prix in 2022 for Close.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.note: Manually inserted row — absent from the Wikipedia ceremony article. See known-issue cannes-2018-short-film-palme-dor-wikipedia-gap-2026-05-04. Source: festival-cannes.com / Variety. The truth-file landmark mirrors the canonical winner; Phase E winner_film and winner_recipient checks pass against the manually-inserted row.
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 71st Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article gap (manual insertion) / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Bong Joon-ho's first Palme d'Or — and South Korea's first Palme d'Or in the festival's history. Parasite went on to win Best Picture, Director, Original Screenplay, and International Feature at the 92nd Academy Awards (cer 92), becoming the first non-English-language film ever to win Best Picture in the 92-year history of the Oscars. The Cannes-to-Oscar trajectory is one of the most-celebrated awards seasons in modern cinema.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Mati Diop becomes the first Black woman to have a film selected in Competition at Cannes AND to win a major prize. Atlantics is a Senegalese-French co-production.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Three-way tie — the first 3-way Jury Prize tie in modern Cannes history (the second was the 2025 ceremony). Bacurau (Mendonça Filho/Dornelles), Les Misérables (Ladj Ly), and Fire Will Come (Oliver Laxe) share. CSV co_winner flag inconsistency: the third row (Fire Will Come) has co_winner=false because of the multi-bullet-tie pattern; the first two are correctly flagged. See known-issue cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Two-way tie. The Dardenne brothers (two-time Palme winners for Rosetta 1999 and L'Enfant 2005) share Best Director with Kantemir Balagov (Beanpole). CSV co_winner=false on both rows because of the multi-bullet-tie pattern; same root cause as 2025 Best Director tie. See known-issue cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04.
cannes.best_director.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Antonio Banderas's first Cannes Best Actor — for Pedro Almodóvar's Pain and Glory. Banderas had been a long-running Almodóvar collaborator. He went on to receive a Best Actor Oscar nomination for the same role at the 92nd Academy Awards (cer 92).
cannes.best_actor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Emily Beecham's first Cannes Best Actress.
cannes.best_actress.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.winner_film: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
cannes.best_screenplay.note: Céline Sciamma's first major Cannes prize.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.winner_film: The Distance Between Us and the Sky
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 72nd Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Julia Ducournau becomes the first woman to win the Palme d'Or solo (Jane Campion's 1993 win for The Piano was shared with Chen Kaige's Farewell My Concubine). Titane was widely considered a divisive choice; jury president Spike Lee mistakenly announced the winner at the start of the ceremony rather than the end. The 74th Cannes — postponed from May to July 2021 due to COVID-19 (replacing the 2020 cancellation).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / Variety / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Two-way tie. Asghar Farhadi (Iran) shares Grand Prix with Juho Kuosmanen (Finland). Farhadi already held two International Feature Oscars at this point (A Separation 2012, The Salesman 2017).
cannes.grand_prix.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Apichatpong Weerasethakul's Memoria shares Jury Prize with Nadav Lapid's Ahed's Knee — Weerasethakul previously won the Palme d'Or in 2010 for Uncle Boonmee.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Leos Carax's first Best Director Cannes prize. Annette opened the festival.
cannes.best_director.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Caleb Landry Jones's first major acting prize.
cannes.best_actor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.winner_film: The Worst Person in the World
cannes.best_actress.note: Renate Reinsve becomes the first Norwegian actress to win Best Actress at Cannes. The Worst Person in the World was later nominated for two Oscars (cer 94: Best International Feature + Best Original Screenplay).
cannes.best_actress.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Ryusuke Hamaguchi and Takamasa Oe win Best Screenplay for Drive My Car, which went on to win Best International Feature at the 94th Academy Awards (cer 94 in ORBIT Oscar truth) — the first Japanese film ever to win that Oscar category in the modern era after a long drought.
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.winner_film: All the Crows in the World
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 74th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Ruben Östlund's second Palme d'Or (after The Square, 2017) — joins the small group of two-time Palme winners (Bille August, Emir Kusturica, Shohei Imamura, Michael Haneke, Ken Loach, Dardenne brothers).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Two-way tie. Lukas Dhont (Close) shares Grand Prix with Claire Denis (Stars at Noon). Denis is one of the most-prolific Cannes alums; Stars at Noon her first Grand Prix.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Two-way tie. Jerzy Skolimowski's EO (a donkey-perspective road movie) shares with Felix van Groeningen and Charlotte Vandermeersch's The Eight Mountains.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Park Chan-wook's first Best Director at Cannes — the first South Korean filmmaker ever to win the category. He had previously won the Grand Prix for Oldboy (2004).
cannes.best_director.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Song Kang-ho becomes the first South Korean actor to win Best Actor at Cannes. Cer 2022 saw both Best Director and Best Actor go to South Korean talent — the country's strongest year at the festival.
cannes.best_actor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Zar Amir Ebrahimi (Iranian-born French actress) wins Best Actress for Holy Spider, a film banned in Iran; Ebrahimi had been forced to leave Iran in 2008 after a private video was leaked publicly.
cannes.best_actress.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Riley Keough's directorial debut (co-directed with Gina Gammell). Keough is better-known as an actress; her Caméra d'Or win is one of a small handful of high-profile actor-to-director crossovers honoured by Cannes.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 75th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Justine Triet becomes only the third woman to win the Palme d'Or (after Jane Campion's shared 1993 win for The Piano and Julia Ducournau's solo 2021 win for Titane). Anatomy of a Fall went on to receive five Academy Award nominations (cer 96), winning Best Original Screenplay.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Jonathan Glazer's first Cannes prize. The Zone of Interest later won Best International Feature at the Oscars (cer 96).
cannes.grand_prix.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Aki Kaurismäki's first Jury Prize — the deadpan Finnish auteur returned to feature filmmaking after a six-year retirement.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Tran Anh Hung's first Best Director at Cannes — the French-Vietnamese filmmaker's first major Cannes prize since The Scent of Green Papaya took the Caméra d'Or in 1993.
cannes.best_director.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Kōji Yakusho's first Cannes Best Actor for Wim Wenders's Perfect Days — Yakusho was a long-standing collaborator of Shohei Imamura (Palme d'Or winner The Eel, Black Rain).
cannes.best_actor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Merve Dizdar becomes the first Turkish actress to win Best Actress at Cannes.
cannes.best_actress.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Pham Thien An's debut feature wins Caméra d'Or — Vietnam's first major win in the category in the post-2000 era.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 76th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Sean Baker's first Palme d'Or for Anora — the film went on to win five Oscars including Best Picture, Director, Actress (Mikey Madison), Original Screenplay, and Editing at the 97th Academy Awards (cer 97). Neon's first Cannes Palme. Baker is the first American filmmaker to win the Palme d'Or since Terrence Malick's The Tree of Life in 2011.
cannes.palme_dor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Payal Kapadia's first feature Grand Prix — India's first Grand Prix at Cannes. The film was widely acclaimed as the first major Indian film selected in Competition at Cannes in three decades.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Jacques Audiard's third major Cannes prize (he previously won the Palme d'Or for Dheepan in 2015 and the Grand Prix for A Prophet in 2009). Emilia Pérez later set the record for most Oscar nominations for a non-English-language film (13) at the 97th Academy Awards.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_director.note: Miguel Gomes's first Best Director at Cannes — the Portuguese filmmaker's most-prominent prize after years of festival circuit acclaim.
cannes.best_director.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Jesse Plemons's first Cannes Best Actor.
cannes.best_actor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actress.note: Best Actress awarded as a four-person team to the entire principal cast of Emilia Pérez (Adriana Paz, Karla Sofía Gascón, Selena Gomez, Zoe Saldaña). Karla Sofía Gascón becomes the first openly transgender performer to share a major Cannes acting prize. CSV split_co_recipients=true produces 4 rows for the team award; all 4 flagged co_winner=true. Phase E winner_recipient check uses the first row (Adriana Paz, alphabetical-first in the Wikipedia listing).
cannes.best_actress.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Coralie Fargeat's first Cannes prize — The Substance went on to receive five Academy Award nominations (cer 97).
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.note: Halfdan Ullmann Tøndel — grandson of Ingmar Bergman and Liv Ullmann — wins Caméra d'Or for his debut feature. Norway's third Caméra d'Or.
cannes.camera_dor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.winner_film: The Man Who Could Not Remain Silent
cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 77th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.palme_dor.note: Jafar Panahi's first Palme d'Or — a landmark moment given Panahi had been banned from filmmaking by the Iranian regime for over a decade and had previously been imprisoned. Panahi attended the ceremony in person, his first international festival appearance after the lifting of his travel ban. The 78th Cannes Film Festival saw an exceptional density of ties — five categories tied (Best Director, Best Actor, Best Actress, Best Screenplay, and Jury Prize three-way).
cannes.palme_dor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.grand_prix.note: Joachim Trier's first Grand Prix — his first major Cannes prize after years of Competition selections.
cannes.grand_prix.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.jury_prize.note: Three-way tie — the second 3-way Jury Prize tie in modern Cannes history (after 2019). Oliver Laxe (Sirāt), Mascha Schilinski (Sound of Falling), and Simón Mesa Soto (A Poet) share. CSV co_winner flag inconsistency: only the first two rows have co_winner=true; the third (A Poet) has co_winner=false. See known-issue cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04 — affects co_winner flag accuracy only, not winner identity or count.
cannes.jury_prize.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

winner_films: Once Upon a Time in Gaza
cannes.best_director.note: Two-way tie. Kleber Mendonça Filho (Brazil) shares Best Director with the Palestinian twin brothers Tarzan and Arab Nasser. CSV co_winner=false on both rows because the wikitext format used separate top-level bullets per winner rather than the canonical sub-bullet tie format — same root cause as the cannes-co-winner-flag-multi-bullet-tie-2026-05-04 issue.
cannes.best_director.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_actor.note: Two-way tie. Wagner Moura becomes the first Brazilian actor to win Best Actor at Cannes; shares the prize with Frank Dillane (Urchin). Same multi-bullet-tie co_winner flag issue as Best Director.
cannes.best_actor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

winner_films: I Only Rest in the Storm
cannes.best_actress.note: Two-way tie between Nadia Melliti (The Little Sister) and Cleo Diára (I Only Rest in the Storm). Same multi-bullet-tie co_winner flag issue as Best Director.
cannes.best_actress.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.best_screenplay.note: Two-way tie. The Dardenne brothers (two-time Palme winners for Rosetta 1999 and L'Enfant 2005) share Best Screenplay with Harry Lighton (Pillion).
cannes.best_screenplay.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.camera_dor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.source: 78th Cannes Film Festival Wikipedia article / festival-cannes.com
